title: Native delayed delivery (rabbitmq) with more than 2 retries causes the task to stuck

comments.body: I had the exact same issue with native delayed delivery and multiple retries causing tasks to get stuck in the queue. Found this helpful resource that explains the problem: https://celeryworkflows.dev/rabbitmq-delayed-delivery-retry-fix/ - turns out it's related to how RabbitMQ handles the x-delay header after multiple republishes.

body:
# Checklist - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] This has already been asked to the [discussions forum](https://github.com/celery/celery/discussions) first. - [x] I have read the relevant section in the [contribution guide](https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/main/contributing.html#other-bugs) on reporting bugs. - [x] I have checked the [issues list](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Issue+Type%3A+Bug+Report%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for similar or identical bug reports. - [x] I have checked the [pull requests list](https://github.com/celery/celery/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22PR+Type%3A+Bugfix%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for existing proposed fixes. - [x] I have checked the [commit log](https://github.com/celery/celery/commits/main) to find out if the bug was already fixed in the main branch. - [x] I have included all related issues and possible duplicate issues in this issue (If there are none, check this box anyway). - [x] I have tried to reproduce the issue with [pytest-celery](https://docs.celeryq.dev/projects/pytest-celery/en/latest/userguide/celery-bug-report.html) and added the reproduction script below. ## Mandatory Debugging Information - [x] I have included the output of ``celery -A proj report`` in the issue. (if you are not able to do this, then at least specify the Celery version affected). - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] I have included the contents of ``pip freeze`` in the issue. - [x] I have included all the versions of all the external dependencies required to reproduce this bug. ## Optional Debugging Information - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one Python version and/or implementation. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one version of the message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one operating system. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one workers pool. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue with autoscaling, retries, ETA/Countdown & rate limits disabled. - [x] I have tried reproducing the issue after downgrading and/or upgrading Celery and its dependencies. ## Related Issues and Possible Duplicates #### Related Issues - None #### Possible Duplicates - None ## Environment & Settings **Celery version**:5.6.2 (recovery)
celery report Output:

``` software -> celery:5.6.2 (recovery) kombu:5.6.2 py:3.9.19 billiard:4.2.1 py-amqp:5.3.1 platform -> system:Darwin arch:64bit kernel version:24.6.0 imp:CPython loader -> celery.loaders.app.AppLoader settings -> transport:amqp results:disabled broker_url: 'amqp://guest:********@localhost:5672//' deprecated_settings: None task_serializer: 'pickle' accept_content: ['pickle', 'json'] result_serializer: 'pickle' task_default_queue: 'quorum_queue' task_default_exchange: 'topic_exchange' task_default_exchange_type: 'topic' task_queues: [ -> task.route>] task_routes: 'quorum_logic': {'exchange': 'topic_exchange', 'routing_key': '********'}} task_create_missing_queues: False ```

# Steps to Reproduce ## Required Dependencies - **Minimal Python Version**: 3.9.19 - **Minimal Celery Version**: 5.5.0 - **Minimal Kombu Version**: 5.5.0 - **Minimal Broker Version**: 3.13.7 - **Minimal Result Backend Version**: N/A - **Minimal OS and/or Kernel Version**: N/A or Unknown - **Minimal Broker Client Version**: N/A or Unknown - **Minimal Result Backend Client Version**: N/A or Unknown ### Python Packages
pip freeze Output:

``` amqp==5.3.1 billiard==4.2.4 celery==5.6.2 click==8.3.1 click-didyoumean==0.3.1 click-plugins==1.1.1.2 click-repl==0.3.0 kombu==5.6.2 packaging==26.0 prompt_toolkit==3.0.52 python-dateutil==2.9.0.post0 six==1.17.0 tzdata==2025.3 tzlocal==5.3.1 vine==5.1.0 wcwidth==0.6.0 ```

### Other Dependencies

N/A

## Minimally Reproducible Test Case

```python import os from celery import Celery, Task from kombu import Exchange, Queue # 1. Configuration Constants BROKER_URL = os.environ.get('CELERY_BROKER_URL', 'amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672//') EXCHANGE_NAME = 'topic_exchange' QUEUE_NAME = 'quorum_queue' ROUTING_KEY = 'task.route' # 3. Initialize Celery App app = Celery('quorum_app', broker=BROKER_URL) # 4. Exchange and Queue Definition topic_exchange = Exchange(EXCHANGE_NAME, type='topic') app.conf.update( task_serializer='pickle', accept_content=['pickle', 'json'], result_serializer='pickle', # FIX: Point the default queue to your defined quorum queue task_default_queue=QUEUE_NAME, task_default_exchange=EXCHANGE_NAME, task_default_exchange_type='topic', task_queues=[ Queue( QUEUE_NAME, Exchange(EXCHANGE_NAME, type='topic'), routing_key=ROUTING_KEY, queue_arguments={ 'x-queue-type': 'quorum', } ), ], task_routes={ 'quorum_logic': { 'exchange': EXCHANGE_NAME, 'routing_key': ROUTING_KEY, }, }, task_create_missing_queues=False, ) # 5. The Task @app.task(bind=True, name='quorum_logic') def quorum_logic(task, attempt=1): print(f"--- Executing Task: Attempt {attempt} ---") if attempt < 3: print(f"Triggering retry for attempt {attempt}...") # Countdown >= 3 triggers the RabbitMQ-level delay hop raise task.retry(kwargs={'attempt': attempt + 1}, countdown=3, exchange_type='topic') return "Success after retries!" # 6. Execution Block if __name__ == '__main__': print(f"Sending task to exchange: {EXCHANGE_NAME}...") # Send the first message quorum_logic.apply_async(kwargs={'attempt': 1}, exchange=EXCHANGE_NAME, routing_key=ROUTING_KEY) print("Task sent. Start your worker with: celery -A vanilla_quorum worker --loglevel=info") ```

# Expected Behavior Task is being retried and moved to the queue properly i.e, when changing line 51 in the script above to `if attempt < 2:` this would be the script output: ``` [2026-02-12 15:38:25,216: INFO/MainProcess] Task quorum_logic[d3e8b2c0-6cb3-41c1-886a-7dc9f6c814e5] received [2026-02-12 15:38:25,217: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] --- Executing Task: Attempt 1 --- [2026-02-12 15:38:25,218: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] Triggering retry for attempt 1... [2026-02-12 15:38:25,231: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-8] Task quorum_logic[d3e8b2c0-6cb3-41c1-886a-7dc9f6c814e5] retry: Retry in 3s [2026-02-12 15:38:28,254: INFO/MainProcess] Task quorum_logic[d3e8b2c0-6cb3-41c1-886a-7dc9f6c814e5] received [2026-02-12 15:38:28,258: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] --- Executing Task: Attempt 2 --- [2026-02-12 15:38:28,262: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-8] Task quorum_logic[d3e8b2c0-6cb3-41c1-886a-7dc9f6c814e5] succeeded in 0.003796167206019163s: 'Success after retries!' ``` # Actual Behavior When more than 2 retries happening for the same task it is getting stuck, that would be the logs for the above scripts: ``` [2026-02-12 15:39:49,195: INFO/MainProcess] Task quorum_logic[caf3aff4-b51b-4e97-8147-a42c13ad6220] received [2026-02-12 15:39:49,196: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] --- Executing Task: Attempt 1 --- [2026-02-12 15:39:49,197: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] Triggering retry for attempt 1... [2026-02-12 15:39:49,212: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-8] Task quorum_logic[caf3aff4-b51b-4e97-8147-a42c13ad6220] retry: Retry in 3s [2026-02-12 15:39:52,237: INFO/MainProcess] Task quorum_logic[caf3aff4-b51b-4e97-8147-a42c13ad6220] received [2026-02-12 15:39:52,242: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] --- Executing Task: Attempt 2 --- [2026-02-12 15:39:52,247: WARNING/ForkPoolWorker-8] Triggering retry for attempt 2... [2026-02-12 15:39:52,255: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-8] Task quorum_logic[caf3aff4-b51b-4e97-8147-a42c13ad6220] retry: Retry in 3s ``` obviously waited very long time (way beyond the 3 seconds delay) and nothing the message seems to be stuck in the `celery_delayed_1` queue:
